one_line_thesis: Swedish industrial serial acquirer of niche subsidiaries — decentralized Pabrai-style model with 2025–2026 margin/FCF turnaround underway; organic sales still negative while unprofitable units are pruned.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Teqnion is a proven decentralized serial acquirer with a strengthening margin story. FY2025 net sales rose 15% on nine bolt-on deals while organic sales fell 5% after deliberate exits of weak units. EBITA grew 36% to 203.1 MSEK at an 11.3% margin, and operating cash flow jumped 92% to 184.6 MSEK. Free cash flow excluding acquisitions was 173.1 MSEK, or 10.08 SEK per share, bridged from Not 27 in the full-tier annual report. Goodwill impairment of 73 MSEK in Q3 2025 and contingent consideration of 195.6 MSEK remind us that deal quality matters. At ~165 SEK, proof-first component value sums t...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

components.label: Acquisition reinvestment runway

method_provenance.label: Owner earnings and reinvestment-return DCF
method_provenance.equation: cash_t = cash_(t-1)*(1+reinvestment*incremental_ROIC); value = PV(distributable cash + terminal owner earnings)

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

components.label: Operating-company portfolio owner cash
components.assumption_summary: Phase 3 direct per-share scaffold. It is a provisional valuation range, not a committee-approved price target.
components.cross_check: Reconcile the range to primary filings, a reproducible owner-cash or NAV bridge, and the identified liability and capital claims before decision use.

components.label: Acquisition reinvestment runway

economic_value.accounting_reference: Existing research and filing-derived valuation inputs. The primary-source reconciliation remains an open evidence gate.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of TEQ.ST, including every currently identified operating claim, financial claim, liability, reserve, and material option.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating and asset claims are valued once; cash, debt, financing, reserves, and other senior claims are separate components or explicitly reserved. No component is intended to overlap another.

valuation_proof.evidence: FY2025 operating CF 184.6 MSEK; FCF ex-acquisitions 173.1 MSEK; cash 209.5 MSEK; contingent consideration 195.6 MSEK; 17,165,756 shares (TEQ.ST/official-reports/annual-reports/2026-03-21 - Årsredovisning 2025.pdf). Proof base 126.4505/sh via owner_cash_or_dividend_discount@1.0.
valuation_proof.adjustment: The low/base/high range incorporates ownership, tax, capital, cycle, timing, and realization uncertainty pending the primary bridge.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key core_engine; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Primary evidence shows that the economic claim, cash conversion, ownership, or required capital is materially worse than this low case.

timeline.label: Q1 2026 interim reported record EBITA margin 14.3% and organic net sales −5%; supports margin-turnaround thesis.
FY2025 annual report: nine acquisitions, goodwill impairment 73 MSEK, board proposes no dividend; FCF ex-acquisitions 173.1 MSEK.
Year-end report 2025 flagged FCF recovery and portfolio cleanup including MSF sale.
Q3 2025 goodwill impairment 73 MSEK signaled past overpayment on at least one unit.
Teqnion UK Ltd established; seven UK acquisitions in FY2025 expand geographic footprint.
Nord/Väst regional structure introduced to simplify portfolio oversight.
MSF sold to suitable buyer — portfolio cleanup of underperforming segment.
Listed on Nasdaq First North Growth Market (IPO April 2019).
Teqnion founded; decentralized buy-and-hold serial acquirer model established.

industry.structure: Fragmented Nordic and UK niche industrial markets; Teqnion holds 38 subsidiaries with local micro-moats. Parent adds capital allocation and M&A pipeline, not centralized operations.
industry.share_shift: Teqnion gained scale via nine FY2025 bolt-ons (including seven in the UK) while pruning unprofitable units (organic −5%). Spiltan remains largest holder at 11.41%.
industry.trend: Margin recovery and FCF inflection after portfolio cleanup; M&A pace remains high with rising net debt and contingent consideration (195.6 MSEK). First North liquidity constrains rerating.

top_claims.claim: Teqnion is a proven decentralized serial acquirer with a strengthening margin story: FY2025 net sales +15% (nine acquisitions), EBITA +36%, organic -5% from deliberate exits, and equity ratio 42.8%. Goodwill impairment 73 MSEK (Q3 2025) flags past overpayment risk. At ~158 SEK, base expected annual return (Lawrence) is
We expect **18.14%** per year over 7 years at **~$158.0** on **$10.08/sh** starting owner cash.
*Primary risk: M&A indiscipline; goodwill impairment SEK 73M (Q3 2025); equity ratio 42.8% and falling.
Organic deterioration beyond deliberate exits (FY2025 organic -5%).

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: M&A indiscipline; goodwill impairment SEK 73M (Q3 2025); equity ratio 42.8% and falling.
Organic deterioration beyond deliberate exits (FY2025 organic -5%).
UK integration failure after seven FY2025 acquisitions.
